events.text: Patricia Hill, 47-year-old Hispanic female, Former smoker (quit 10 years ago). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with Invasive ductal carcinoma of the Left breast. Clinical staging: T2N1M1, AJCC Stage IV. ICD-10: C50.911. Stage IV HER2-positive breast cancer with liver and bone metastases.
Bone scan performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 + Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w x 6 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 + Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w x 6 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 + Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w x 6 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 + Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w x 6 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade.
Clinical visit for results review after first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade.
Initiated maintenance HER2-directed therapy: Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w maintenance. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated maintenance HER2-directed therapy: Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w maintenance. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 2 diarrhea. Managed with loperamide 4 mg then 2 mg after each loose stool. Stool studies negative.
Initiated maintenance HER2-directed therapy: Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w maintenance. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.
Initiated maintenance HER2-directed therapy: Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w maintenance. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Initiated maintenance HER2-directed therapy: Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w maintenance. Cycle 5. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated maintenance HER2-directed therapy: Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w maintenance. Cycle 6. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 6 assessment.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after maintenance HER2-directed therapy.
Clinical visit for results review after maintenance HER2-directed therapy.
Initiated third-line HER2-directed therapy: Tucatinib + Trastuzumab + Capecitabine. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated third-line HER2-directed therapy: Tucatinib + Trastuzumab + Capecitabine. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 3 neutropenia. Treated with G-CSF (filgrastim 5 mcg/kg daily x 5 days). CBC monitored closely.
Initiated third-line HER2-directed therapy: Tucatinib + Trastuzumab + Capecitabine. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated third-line HER2-directed therapy: Tucatinib + Trastuzumab + Capecitabine. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 3 neutropenia. Treated with G-CSF (filgrastim 5 mcg/kg daily x 5 days). CBC monitored closely.
Initiated third-line HER2-directed therapy: Tucatinib + Trastuzumab + Capecitabine. Cycle 5. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after third-line HER2-directed therapy.
Clinical visit for results review after third-line HER2-directed therapy.
Brain MRI with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
BONE SCAN REPORT

Patient: Patricia Hill
MRN: patient_aec91c8cad76
Date of Study: 2022-08-08

CLINICAL INDICATION: Invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Whole body bone scintigraphy performed following IV injection of 25 mCi Tc-99m MDP.

FINDINGS:
Multiple foci of increased radiotracer uptake in the axial skeleton consistent with widespread osseous metastatic disease. Approximately 13 lesions identified.

IMPRESSION:
1. Known breast malignancy with hepatic and osseous metastases.
2. Liver lesions as described.

Electronically signed by: David Kim, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Patricia Hill
MRN: patient_aec91c8cad76
Specimen ID: S2022-85151
Date of Procedure: 2022-08-10
Date of Report: 2022-08-14

SPECIMEN: Left breast, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Invasive ductal carcinoma, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 3.4 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
ER: Negative (0%)
PR: Negative (0%)
HER2 IHC: 3+ (positive)
Ki-67: 45%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Patricia Hill - Left breast" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 3.4 x 2.4 x 1.7 cm. The cut surface reveals a indurated tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A7.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Breast Surgery

Patient: Patricia Hill
MRN: patient_aec91c8cad76
Date: 2022-08-21
Provider: Dr. Carlos Mendez

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Hill is a 47-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast, IV. Patient presented with symptoms prompting workup. Stage IV HER2-positive breast cancer with liver and bone metastases. Pathology confirmed invasive ductal carcinoma. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports progressive dyspnea and cough x 6 weeks.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- hyperlipidemia
- gastroesophageal reflux disease

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Right knee arthroscopy (age 45)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Never smoker
- Alcohol: Former heavy drinker, quit 5 years ago
- Occupation: Accountant
- Lives with spouse and adult children

FAMILY HISTORY:
- No significant family history.

ALLERGIES: NKDA

MEDICATIONS:
- Atorvastatin 40 mg daily
- Omeprazole 20 mg daily

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: fatigue, intermittent headaches, intermittent nausea, unintentional weight loss. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 125/65, HR 77, RR 18, Temp 98.1F, SpO2 96% on RA, Wt 65.2 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 6.4, Hgb 13.0, Plt 227, Cr 0.9

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
47-year-old female with IV invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast. initial oncology consultation.

1. Invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast, Stage IV
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Helen Park, MD
Breast Surgery

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Patricia Hill
MRN: patient_aec91c8cad76
Date of Report: 2022-08-31
Specimen: Left breast (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: Invasive ductal carcinoma
Panel: Tempus xT (468 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 2.5 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  ERBB2 Amplification (copy number 12) - Amplification [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  PIK3CA p.H1047R - Missense (Exon 20)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CHEK2 p.I157T - VUS
  PALB2 p.L939W - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - ERBB2 amplification: FDA-approved HER2-directed therapies include trastuzumab, pertuzumab, T-DXd, tucatinib.
  - PIK3CA H1047R: Alpelisib may be considered in HR+/HER2- setting.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 775x coverage.
Tumor content: 28%.

Electronically signed by: Andrew Patel, MD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 6.6, Hgb 14.5, Plt 164, Cr 0.83

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
47-year-old female with IV invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast. cycle 2 assessment.

1. Invasive ductal carcinoma - cycle 2 assessment
   - Proceed with next cycle as planned
   - Tumor markers trending favorably
2. Symptom management
   - Pain well controlled on current regimen
3. Follow-up in 2 weeks

Electronically signed by: Helen Park, MD

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Patricia Hill
MRN: patient_aec91c8cad76
Date of Study: 2022-12-04

CLINICAL INDICATION: Invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast. restaging after first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Few pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 1.5 cm in the left lower lobe. Consistent with metastatic disease.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: 2 hypodense lesions consistent with metastases, largest in segment 7 measuring 3.6 cm. New finding.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: Multiple lytic osseous lesions involving thoracic and lumbar spine consistent with osseous metastatic disease.

IMPRESSION:
1. Known breast malignancy with hepatic and osseous metastases.
2. Liver lesions decreased in size, partial response.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Breast Surgery

   - Labs today show adequate counts for treatment
2. Symptom management
   - Continue antiemetic regimen
3. Follow-up in 6 weeks

Patient: Patricia Hill
MRN: patient_aec91c8cad76
Date of Study: 2023-05-05

CLINICAL INDICATION: Invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast. restaging after maintenance HER2-directed therapy.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Few pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 0.6 cm in the left upper lobe. Consistent with metastatic disease.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: 2 hypodense lesions consistent with metastases, largest in segment 8 measuring 2.6 cm. New finding.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: Multiple mixed lytic-sclerotic osseous lesions involving pelvis and bilateral iliac bones consistent with osseous metastatic disease.

IMPRESSION:
1. Known breast malignancy with hepatic and osseous metastases.
2. Liver lesions decreased in size, partial response.

Electronically signed by: David Kim, MD
Department of Radiology

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Few pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 1.4 cm in the right lower lobe. Consistent with metastatic disease.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: 3 hypodense lesions consistent with metastases, largest in segment 4a measuring 3.9 cm. New finding.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: Multiple lytic osseous lesions involving thoracic and lumbar spine consistent with osseous metastatic disease.

IMPRESSION:
1. Known breast malignancy with hepatic and osseous metastases.
2. Liver lesions decreased in size, partial response.
